channel.title: Markdown on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Markdown on emsenn.net

item.title: OpenClaw Memory
item.description:
OpenClaw memory is the Markdown-based memory layer in an OpenClaw workspace. Its source of truth is written files on disk rather than session-local model state.

¶Structure

The official memory docs describe two default durable layers:

  • MEMORY.md for curated long-term memory
  • memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md for dated logs and running context

Memory search tools are provided by the active memory plugin, but the files remain primary. Search augments retrieval; it does not replace the underlying Markdown record.

item.title: OpenProse
item.description:
OpenProse is a markdown-first workflow format and OpenClaw plugin for explicit, reusable agent programs. In OpenClaw it ships as a plugin that installs a skill pack plus a /prose slash command.

¶Role

Programs live in .prose files and can spawn multiple sub-agents with explicit control flow. The official docs describe research and synthesis, incident triage, code review, and content pipelines as core use cases.

¶Scope

OpenProse sits above individual tools and below general agent behavior. It is the program layer for repeatable multi-step workflows.

item.title: Frontmatter Metadata
item.description:
Frontmatter is a YAML header that records metadata for a markdown file.

¶Purpose

  • Identify the document (id, title or name).
  • Record provenance (authors, date-created).
  • Provide routing data (tags, dependencies, references).

¶Consistency

Keep metadata stable and machine-readable so tools can query a vault reliably.

item.title: Markdown Files
item.description:
Markdown files are plain-text documents that mix human-readable prose with a small set of structural markers.

¶Structure

  • A file may begin with YAML frontmatter.
  • Headings provide a hierarchy of sections.
  • Links connect files and build a network of references.

¶Data view

Treat each file as a node with:

  • an identifier,
  • a title,
  • tags and dependencies,
  • outbound links and references.

item.title: Markdown Vault Workflows
item.description:
GnoponEmacs should make it easy to navigate and edit a markdown vault.

¶Core workflows

  • Open a vault root and browse by file, tag, or link.
  • Jump between linked files and maintain backlinks.
  • Inspect and edit frontmatter with minimal friction.

¶Semiotic extensions

  • Surface interaction terms from headings and links.
  • Generate or open vault index files for overview navigation.

item.title: Markdown Vaults
item.description:
A vault is a collection of markdown files treated as a linked knowledge base.

¶Vault properties

  • Files are nodes; links are edges.
  • Frontmatter supplies stable identifiers and metadata.
  • Index files summarize the vault and provide entry points.

¶Vault hygiene

  • Keep id values unique and stable.
  • Normalize tags and dependencies.
  • Track missing or broken links for repair.
